capabilities: Implementing vector indexes using Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW) and Inverted File (IVF) techniques to optimize search performance.

procedure.action: Select the index type by evaluating dataset characteristics and query patterns; choose HNSW for high recall and IVF for large-scale datasets with frequent updates.

Initialize index parameters by setting HNSW efConstruction and M values or IVF nlist and nprobe values based on dataset size and desired recall rate by loading environment-specific values from config file; assert all required keys are present via config_validate(); assert config_validation_error_count < 1.

Compile the index using the chosen library (e.g., FAISS or Annoy) by loading the dataset and executing the index construction function with configured parameters by compiling with deterministic build flags; treat all compiler warnings as errors (exit_code > 0 on any warning); assert output binary hash is stable across consecutive builds.
Benchmark index performance by running a set of queries and measuring recall and query time; ensure recall > 90% and query_latency_p95 < 100ms.

Profile index parameters iteratively by adjusting efConstruction, M, nlist, and nprobe values; rerun benchmarks to validate improvements by profiling the target path and ranking bottlenecks by duration_ms; assert p95_latency improves by >= 20% after applying the highest-impact optimization.

summary: A structured approach to implementing approximate nearest neighbor vector indexes using HNSW and IVF techniques for efficient search.
title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Vector Index Implementation

failureConditions: Poor search performance when index parameters are misconfigured, causing high query latency and low recall rates.
Increased memory usage when HNSW parameters are set too high, leading to resource exhaustion and potential system crashes.
Inaccurate search results when IVF nlist is too low, causing insufficient partitioning and high false positive rates.
metrics: all required keys are present via config_validate()
output binary hash is stable across consecutive builds
p95_latency improves by >= 20% after applying the highest-impact optimization
successConditions: memory_usage < 80% of available RAM during index build
